title: Hong Kong warns of fake tokens posing as HSBC’s stablecoin
summary: 1. HSBC and Anchorpoint Financial both clarified that they have not launched any stablecoins in Hong Kong, but plan to do so this year.
author: Timmy Shen

title: Twenty One Capital’s Jack Mallers Argues BTC’s Proof of Reserves Outclasses Gold at Bitcoin 2026
summary:
1. At Bitcoin 2026, Jack Mallers, chief executive of the $3.3 billion bitcoin treasury firm Twenty One Capital, stated that bitcoin has a structural advantage over gold, pointing to its onchain proof of reserves as something no gold custodian can replicate.
2. Key Takeaways: Twenty One Capital CEO Jack Mallers says bitcoin’s onchain auditability gives it an […]
author: Shiraz Jagati

title: Fenbushi Capital deposits $7.13M in ETH to Binance
summary: Blockchain-focused venture capital firm Fenbushi Capital withdrew 3,063 ETH, worth $7.13 million, from Aave and subsequently deposited the funds to Binance, Lookonchain reported. Such deposits to exchanges are often interpreted as a precursor to selling.
author: @CoinnessGL

title: Crypto Regulation Crossroads: Certik Report Maps Divergent Global Paths
summary:
1. The findings of a new study reveals a global shift from drafting crypto policies to aggressive active enforcement.
2. Key Takeaways: Certik reports EMEA fines hit $168.2M in H1 2025, a 767% surge as regulators shift to active supervision.
3. The EU’s MiCA framework is driving major exchanges to France and Ireland by providing 100% legal certainty.
author: Terence Zimwara

title: Ripple Positions Custody as Core to Institutional Crypto Growth
summary:
1. Ripple custody strategy gains traction as European institutions move into production environments requiring secure digital asset infrastructure.
2. The shift positions custody systems as the operational foundation for scaling institutional crypto use cases across payments, tokenization, and treasury management.
3. Key Takeaways: Ripple highlights custody as core infrastructure driving institutional crypto adoption across Europe.
author: Kevin Helms

title: CFTC pulls Wisconsin into fight over prediction market jurisdiction
summary: 1. The US Commodity Futures Trading Commission has sued Wisconsin in the agency’s fifth lawsuit against a US state to assert jurisdiction over prediction markets.

title: Ethereum ICO whale moves $23 million in ETH after 10-year dormancy
summary: 1. The Ethereum whale address received the ETH on July 30, 2015, after participating in the ICO event with $3,100.

title: Bitcoin rises to $77,000 ahead of Fed decision as Trump preps for lengthy Hormuz block
summary:
1. Bitcoin is sitting almost still while the rest of the majors give back gains and oil pushes above $111 on reports of an extended U.S.
2. naval blockade against Iran.

title: Robinhood dips as Q1 earnings miss and crypto revenue, volume fall nearly 50%
summary: 1. Robinhood’s Q1 earnings per share and revenue figures missed industry expectations, sending the company’s shares down nearly 10%.

title: Canada moves to ban crypto ATMs, labeling them as ‘primary method’ for fraud
summary: 1. The government said it aims to shut down a 'primary method' used by scammers to defraud victims and facilitate money laundering.

title: Bitcoin’s recent rally is largely fueled by Strategy purchases: Bitwise's Hougan
summary: 1. While Bitcoin exchange-traded funds and whale buying have contributed to the recent Bitcoin rally, the key driver has been consistent buying by Michael Saylor's treasury firm, Strategy, according to Bitwise's chief investment officer.

title: How one venture firm is investing in an increasingly fragmented world

title: What are the best communities for Ethereum Builders?
summary:
1. Hello everyone.
2. We are building a lending protocol with a focus on RWAs on Ethereum.
3. We wonder to ask what are the best places, communities and groups to connect with other builders, stakeholders, KOLs and users on the Ethereum ecosystem.
